activities_projects.title: Constitution Makers Research Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of leaders in drafting the Indian Constitution
steps: Read about the making of the Indian Constitution
Identify key members of the Constituent Assembly such as Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, Jawaharlal Nehru, and Rajendra Prasad
Write their roles in the drafting process
Prepare a chart showing their contributions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of the Constituent Assembly and its leaders
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of research and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: States Reorganisation Map Project
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how linguistic demands led to the formation of new states
steps: Study the States Reorganisation Act of 1956
Mark states formed or reorganized based on language
Color each state and label its official language
Write a short note explaining why linguistic states were created
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of language in administrative reorganization
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct map work and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Refugee Rehabilitation Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the challenges faced after Partition and how refugees were resettled
steps: Read about the migration during Partition
List problems faced by refugees such as housing, employment, and security
Study how the government helped them settle
Write a short report summarizing your findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the social and economic impact of Partition
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of explanation and clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Five Year Plans Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Examine the goals and achievements of India's early Five-Year Plans
steps: Study the objectives of the First and Second Five-Year Plans
Identify focus areas such as agriculture, irrigation, and industry
Prepare a comparative chart showing their goals and achievements
Discuss how they contributed to India's development
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand planned economic development in independent India
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparison and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Green Revolution Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of the Green Revolution on Indian agriculture
steps: Read about the Green Revolution and the introduction of high-yield variety seeds
Identify regions where it had major impact
List benefits such as increased food production
Discuss challenges like environmental issues
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand both positive and negative effects of agricultural modernization

activities_projects.title: Language and Identity Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Explore how language influences identity and politics in India
steps: Identify different languages spoken across India
Study movements demanding linguistic states
Discuss why language became an important political issue
Write a summary of your discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how language shaped political and regional identity
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of reasoning and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Science and Technology Development Timeline
activities_projects.objective: Understand India's progress in science and technology after independence
steps: List important institutions such as ISRO and DRDO
Identify key achievements like satellite launches and nuclear research
Create a timeline showing major milestones
Add brief descriptions for each event
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize India's advancements in science and technology
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and organization of information (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role of Women in Nation Building
activities_projects.objective: Understand the contributions of women in post-independence India
steps: Identify notable women leaders and reformers
Study their contributions in politics, education, or social reform
Prepare a short report about their work
Share findings with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students appreciate the role of women in shaping modern India
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Urban and Rural Development Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Analyze development differences between rural and urban areas
steps: Observe facilities available in your town or village
List services such as roads, schools, hospitals, and markets
Compare these with facilities in nearby urban areas
Write a short comparative report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand uneven development patterns in India

activities_projects.title: Debate on Development Policies
activities_projects.objective: Encourage critical thinking about India's development strategies
steps: Divide the class into two groups
Choose a topic such as 'Industrial development vs agricultural development'
Each group prepares arguments
Conduct a classroom debate
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical and communication skills while understanding development issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and strength of arguments (10 marks)
